title: event — model relationships
description: Event sits below Tenant + Organization + optionally Branch + optionally Season. Its outbound relations reach up to those parents; its inbound relations arrive from Session (Wave 3b), teams::EventTeam (Wave 2b nullable), athlete-enrollment (Wave 3+). Every FK is same-tenant. Event NEVER carries application_id / region_id / scope_node_id.

internal.description: Facilities attached to this event via the event_facilities pivot.

internal.constraints: WHERE is_primary=true AND deleted_at IS NULL
internal.description: The primary facility for this event.

outbound.description: Required. RESTRICT because archiving an org with active events must fail loudly.
Optional. When set, event is single-branch. RESTRICT — branch closure with active events triggers the branch-closure listener (writes warning, tenant decides how to handle).
Optional. Stand-alone events (awards ceremonies, camp openings not tied to a season) have season_id = NULL.
CASCADE — pivot lifetime bounded by the event.
RESTRICT — cannot retire a facility with active event attachments.

inbound.description: Downstream domain models compose the BelongsToEvent trait shipped here. They store the linkage as event_id.
examples: session::Session.event_id — nullable. A session may or may not be part of an event's schedule (Wave 3b).
teams::EventTeam.event_id — nullable in Wave 2b; Wave 3 migration makes required + FK-constrained. Team's participation in an event.
athlete-enrollment::AthleteEnrollment.event_id — Wave 3+. Some enrollments target a specific event rather than a season.
inbound.onDelete: RESTRICT by convention. EventObserver refuses to archive an Event while ANY inbound row still references it (returns EVENT_HAS_ACTIVE_REGISTRATIONS 409). The FK RESTRICT is the fallback.

notes: Event is NOT a scope consumer. scope_node_id on events is forbidden per tenancy-columns.md §4.
Forbidden columns per tenancy-columns.md §5: application_id (Event is NOT one of the 8 rows in §2), region_id (Events cascade through branch.region_id), scope_node_id.
Event does not carry parent_id — events are flat. A tournament with prelims + finals is TWO Events, related via the event_series metadata field OR future event_relations pivot.

The event_facilities pivot exists because ResourceBookings (facility module) already own the per-slot reservation; event_facilities is the composition contract — one row per facility per event, not per slot. The ReconcileEventFacilityBookingsJob keeps event_facilities and resource_bookings in sync.
